warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: The use of food additives must comply with the specified national standard. Specific limits and conditions are detailed within the standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the online database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these permitted maximum levels or guideline levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.parameter: Approved food additives and their conditions of use
rules.sourceTitle: Additives Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Verification required against the Union list of food additives in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Competence of laboratories conducting shelf-life studies (challenge tests and durability studies) for Listeria monocytogenes
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Laboratories must comply with food safety criteria in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 and follow the EURL Lm Guidance Document for competence evaluation.
rules.evidenceQuote: The EURL Lm Guidance Document to evaluate the competence of laboratories implementing challenge tests and durability studies related to Listeria monocytogenes in ready-to-eat foods aims to set up a harmonised approach to evaluate the competence of laboratories conducting shelf-life studies (challenge tests and durability studies), in order to comply with the food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

rules.parameter: Food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, toxins, metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine) and process hygiene criteria
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Compliance required with Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites... These criteria define the acceptability of a product or a batch of food applicable to products placed on the market. In addition, this Regulation lays down certain process hygiene criteria...

rules.parameter: Sampling procedures for Listeria monocytogenes on surfaces of processing areas and equipment
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Food business operators must follow the Guidelines on sampling.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Guidelines on sampling the food processing areas and equipment describes sampling procedures to be performed by food business operators manufacturing RTE food which may pose a L. monocytogenes risk for public health in order to detect L. monocytogenes on the surfaces of RTE food processing areas and equipment.

rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Follow the Technical guidance document for conducting durability studies and challenge tests.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Technical guidance document on shelf-life studies for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods provides specialised laboratories with detailed and practical information on how to conduct shelf-life studies (especially durability studies and challenge tests) for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 Halal food - Part 1: General requirements for Halal food
rules.notes: This standard specifies the general requirements for Halal food, which may include labelling for Halal certification.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.notes: This standard specifies the general requirements for the labelling of pre-packaged foodstuffs.

rules.parameter: Optional shelf life for food products

rules.notes: General labelling requirements for prepackaged food, including but not limited to ingredient lists, nutritional information, net content, manufacturer details, and potentially allergen declarations, must be verified against the full text of this technical regulation.

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products thereof

rules.notes: These are specific allergens for which labeling is recommended.

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite
rules.unit: milligrams per kilogram of food (for sulphite)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Sulphite labeling is mandatory if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Food allergen labeling is required for prepackaged foods that contain any of the specified 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Food allergen labeling is recommended for prepackaged foods that contain certain additional allergens as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Prepackaged food products must comply with revised regulations on nutrition claims.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A precautionary allergen labeling statement is suggested if there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination in the production process.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific additive limits and permitted uses require verification from official regulations under 'วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร'.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific limits for contaminants, toxic substances, and veterinary drug residues require verification from official regulations. Draft announcements regarding these limits, including ASEAN MRLs, are under consideration.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน, มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง, อาหารที่มียาสัตว์ตกค้าง, อาหารที่มีสารพิษตกค้าง, (ร่าง) ค่าปริมาณสารพิษตกค้างสูงสุดของอาเซียน (ASEAN MRLs)

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Certain foods are prohibited from production, import, or sale. Verification of prohibited food lists is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific pathogenic microorganism limits require verification from official regulations under 'มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค'.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Food additives and color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively) or evaluated by FEMA/JECFA for flavoring substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: GRAS substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184) or in the inventory of GRAS Notices.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, must be listed in 21 CFR Part 181.
rules.evidenceQuote: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) and labeled as 'PROHIBITED' or 'PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS").

rules.limit: Milk, Eggs, Fish, Crustacean shellfish, Tree nuts, Peanuts, Wheat, Soybeans, Sesame
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The FASTER Act (effective January 1, 2023) declared sesame as the 9th major food allergen. These allergens account for 90 percent of food allergies and serious allergic reactions in the U.S.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.foodCategory: Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces (as ingredients in processed foods, seasonings, snack foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Guidance Level for 3-MCPD.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.foodCategory: Foods (general), Brazil nuts, Peanuts and peanut products, Pistachio nuts
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Action Level for total Aflatoxins.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Brazil nuts Action Level 20 ppb; Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb; Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb; Aflatoxins, total Pistachio nuts Action Level 20 ppb

rules.parameter: Chemical Contaminants (Tolerances, Action Levels, Guidance Levels)
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Manufacturers must check the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool for specific tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Contaminant levels listed in the transparency tool include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels.

rules.limit: Not required by law, must be truthful and not misleading
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Advisory statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable 'cross-contact' if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes and taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The 'Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food' rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling. A separate rule (August 12, 2020) establishes compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling, which helps consumers, especially those living with celiac disease, be confident that items labeled “gluten-free” meet a defined standard for gluten content.

rules.foodCategory: packaged foods and beverages, dietary supplements
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: FALCPA requires foods or ingredients containing a 'major food allergen' to be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source. This applies to most packaged food products, including dietary supplements.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.foodCategory: packaged foods and beverages, dietary supplements
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label either in parentheses following the ingredient name (e.g., 'lecithin (soy)') or immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement (e.g., 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.').
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.foodCategory: packaged foods and beverages, dietary supplements
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The type of tree nut (e.g., almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (e.g., bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (e.g., crab, lobster, shrimp) must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food at a concentration of ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents, including sodium bisulfite, are allowed as food ingredients. But due in part to adverse reactions to them, such as asthma in sensitive individuals, they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.parameter: Food safety compliance for imported goods
rules.limit: Verification required against national food safety standards
rules.sourceTitle: 28/2026/TT-BCT Ban hành Danh mục các mặt hàng nhập khẩu (kèm theo mã số HS) thực hiện kiểm tra nhà nước về an toàn thực phẩm thuộc trách nhiệm quản lý nhà nước của Bộ Công Thương
rules.notes: This circular mandates state inspection for food safety for listed imported goods, implying compliance with relevant national food safety standards (e.g., for additives, contaminants, microbiology, allergens, etc.). The specific list of goods and their HS codes would need to be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: Danh mục các mặt hàng nhập khẩu (...) thực hiện kiểm tra nhà nước về an toàn thực phẩm
